svg.text: The paradigm shift — apps were products you visited. Capabilities are services you invoke.
The old world — apps as destinations
An app is: a URL · a codebase · a team · a frontend · a backend
a security review · an onboarding doc · a bookmark
→ User must remember to check it
→ Data from each app is siloed — no composition possible
→ 30 apps = 30 security postures, 30 audit trails, 30 experiences
The new world — capabilities as services
A capability is: a manifest · an API · a domain agent
→ Capability is discovered by intent, not URL
→ Work arrives at the user via push
→ Data composable across all systems at query time
→ One security posture, one audit trail, one experience
→ New capability: publish manifest → instantly discoverable
AOP extracted cross-cutting concerns from classes. Pangaea extracts them from the entire organisation's software ecosystem.
What the one app solves — beyond saving team time
Realistic. Not every interaction, every day.
are where the time is recovered.
✓ One audit trail across all systems
The org gets a nervous system. Not just
a better app. A way to see itself.
✓ In agentic era: build reasoning, not UI
Teams are freed to do what only they
can do. The rest is platform.
Chained requests — five systems, one surface, one sentence
"Show me Angie Chen's staffing history, learning profile, feedback for this year and last, current goals, and GitHub contributions"
One sentence. Five systems. User does not know or care which systems exist.
5 OBO tokens · 5 syscalls · 1 receipt · ProvenanceFooter shows all sources
Actions derived from combined payload: Write feedback · Suggest for role · Nominate cert renewal
Custom dashboards + pinned views — user-owned, live data
A saved view = a saved intent composition. Not a data snapshot.
When opened: syscalls fire live. Data is always current.
User pins quick-actions, composite queries, capability shortcuts.
View definitions shareable: "here's the team lead dashboard".
Permissions enforced at syscall time — not at view save time.
📌 "My team" — staffing + learning gaps + open roles
📌 "Pending approvals" — all actions awaiting me across all systems
📌 "Angie Chen profile" — saved composite query, one tap
📌 "Team health" — evals + feedback + goals + github · all reports
📌 "Engineering learning" — my path + team gaps + new content
The federated data graph — the northstar for the data layer
Data never moves to Pangaea. The graph describes where it lives and how it connects. Queries traverse and pull in transit.
Teams build capabilities + agents. Platform builds experience + trust + graph. Users get one surface that knows everything they're permitted to know.
